task_completion.description: The agent opened the SRS document to view the requirements.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SRS document is visible in the application's main view or a tab, displaying a list of requirements with attributes such as Release, Priority, and Status.

task_completion.description: The agent removed existing verification links from v2.0 requirements where the linked test case did not match the requirement topic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The verification links column or section for the affected v2.0 requirements no longer displays the incorrect test case IDs.

task_completion.description: The agent created new verification links connecting the missing or corrected v2.0 requirements to the matching test cases in the TESTS document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The verification links column or section for the affected v2.0 requirements shows newly added links to test cases whose subjects match the requirement topics.

task_completion.description: The agent updated the Status to 'Blocked' for the fixed v2.0 requirements that have a 'High' Priority.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Status column for the High priority v2.0 requirements that were modified now displays 'Blocked'.

task_completion.description: The agent updated the Status to 'Reviewed' for the fixed v2.0 requirements that do not have a 'High' Priority.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Status column for the non-High priority v2.0 requirements that were modified now displays 'Reviewed'.

task_completion.description: The agent saved all modifications made to the project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save button is clicked, a save confirmation is shown, or there is no unsaved changes indicator (such as an asterisk next to the project or document name).

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the requirements management application GUI rather than using a terminal or script to modify project files directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor clicking on application menus, rows, and dropdowns, rather than a terminal window executing scripts.

integrity.description: The agent actually opened or searched the TESTS document to find the matching test cases.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the TESTS document open in a tab or window, or a search/link dialog being used to browse and select test cases.

integrity.description: The agent did not open the underlying project files in a text editor to manually change the raw data.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like VS Code, Notepad, Vim) are visible modifying the project's raw JSON, XML, or database files.
